title: Let's Go - A Primer
notes: Requested voice: Professor like Richard Feynman, but an expert in Golang. Requested look: light theme, minimal. Brief: Write a book on Go for someone starting Go programming; guide their learning from first principles. Explain concepts very simply and straightforwardly; don't spend too much time on syntax (cover syntax in the cheatsheet instead). Aim for one concept per page, unless a concept needs multiple pages. Readers are interested in Go-specific interesting features — research these yourself. Include 2D animations where they would help.
